meta.note: Each object in `events` powers one card on events.html and one detail page at event.html?id=<id>. Photo galleries support per-photo caption + description and open in a lightbox.

events.title: The Trusted AI Executive Forum
events.summary: Privacy, security and scalable innovation — an afternoon high above Biscayne Bay with the executives deciding how AI actually ships inside the enterprise.
events.tagline: Innovation moves fast. Trust moves markets.
description: High above Biscayne Bay, in the Miami Downtown Development Authority's offices at the Citigroup Center, we gathered a room of CISOs, chief privacy and data officers, and compliance leaders for an afternoon built around one question: how do you scale AI without losing the trust of your customers, your team and your board?

The answer ran through every conversation. A keynote from Patrick Doliny, the 2026 CSO Cybersecurity Award recipient, set the tone, and the sessions that followed — with voices from PrivacyPal, Key Focus Group, Plug and Play Ventures and the Miami DDA — got specific fast about where AI quietly breaks on sensitive data, and what it takes to put it to work anyway. PrivacyPal CEO Jason Melo made the case for treating privacy as the thing that unlocks AI rather than the thing that blocks it.

It was an intentionally small room — around fifty senior leaders who stayed for the part that doesn't fit on a slide: the introductions, the side conversations, and the partnerships that started taking shape over the skyline. Proof that in Miami, trust really does move markets.
highlights: Keynote from Patrick Doliny, 2026 CSO Cybersecurity Award recipient
Sessions on privacy, security and scaling AI with trust intact — including PrivacyPal CEO Jason Melo
An intimate room of around fifty CISOs, privacy, data and compliance leaders
Hosted high above Biscayne Bay at the Miami DDA in the Citigroup Center
In partnership with Key Focus Group and the Miami Downtown Development Authority

events.title: PrivacyPal Launch
events.summary: We took the wraps off PrivacyPal in front of founders, operators and the South Florida tech community — the night the privacy layer for AI went public.
events.tagline: The night the privacy layer for AI went public.
description: PrivacyPal launched at The LAB Miami in front of a packed room of founders, operators, investors and the South Florida tech community. After months of heads-down building, it was the first time we showed the product live: real prompts, real sensitive data, and Privacy Twins swapping it out in real time before it ever reached a model.

The energy in the room said everything. People don't get excited about privacy software very often — but they get excited about finally being able to say yes to AI without handing over the keys. That's the night we knew we'd built something people had been waiting for.
highlights: First public, live demo of on-device Privacy Twins
Founders, operators and investors from across South Florida
Hosted at The LAB Miami in Wynwood

gallery.alt: PrivacyPal Launch at The LAB Miami
gallery.caption: Launch night at The LAB
gallery.description: The room filled up early — founders, operators and friends of the team came out to see the privacy layer for AI go live for the first time.

gallery.caption: Live on stage
gallery.description: Walking the audience through a real prompt, with real sensitive data, and Privacy Twins doing the work in real time.

gallery.caption: The after-party conversations
gallery.description: The best part of any launch — the conversations that keep going long after the demo ends.
